label: Days Since El Mencho Killed
deltaNote: O3 reportedly named successor. El Pepe (logistics operator who led surveillance to El Mencho) arrested Mar 16 in Tlajomulco, Jalisco. Memo (senior financial/logistics lieutenant) arrested Mar 17 in Mexico City. 4 CJNG members arrested in Tulum Mar 15.

label: U.S. Bounty on El Mencho
source: U.S. State Dept Narcotics Rewards Program — Dec 2024
deltaNote: Largest bounty ever for a drug trafficker at time of increase; contributed to Feb 22 operation

label: Mexican States with CJNG Presence
contestNote: Estimates range 25–29 states depending on methodology; post-Mencho fragmentation may alter count

label: Colima Homicide Rate (per 100k)
deltaNote: Still highest of any Mexican state; 5× national average. Post-Mencho succession violence expected to push rate higher in 2026.

label: CJNG Assets Subject to U.S. Forfeiture
source: U.S. DOJ — El Menchito Sentencing, Mar 7, 2025
contestNote: Order targets Rubén Oseguera González; actual recovery expected to be far lower

label: Mexico Homicides Declined in 2025
contestNote: 23,374 total homicides in 2025 (17.5/100k — lowest since 2016). Analysts caution decline may reflect criminal consolidation, not genuine peace. Post-Mencho fragmentation expected to reverse gains.
deltaNote: Fourth consecutive year of decline nationally; analysts warn of reversal in 2026

label: CJNG HVTs Extradited/Captured
source: U.S. DOJ / FGR / SEDENA / La Silla Rota — Mar 2026
deltaNote: El Pepe (El Mencho surveillance chain logistics, Mar 16); Memo (senior financial/logistics lieutenant, Mar 17); 4 CJNG members in Tulum (Mar 15); El Doble R arrested Mar 11. Sustained strategic pursuit beyond Tapalpa operation.

label: Annual Cost of Violence to Mexico Economy
source: Vision of Humanity Mexico Peace Index — 2024
contestNote: Estimate equals ~18% of Mexican GDP; rose for first time since 2019 despite national homicide decline. CJNG fragmentation expected to increase cost in 2026.
